mnemonics.title: Remember Four Colour Theorem Year
mnemonics.content: GUTHRIE (18)52 to APPEL HAKEN (19)76 - That's 124 years of waiting!

mnemonics.title: Euler's Formula Memory
mnemonics.content: V - E + F = 2 - Very Easy Formula (V=Very, E=Easy, F=Formula)

mnemonics.title: Heawood Number
mnemonics.content: H = floor((7 + sqrt(1+48g))/2) - Heawood's formula for maximum colours on surface of genus g

acronyms.usage: Used to determine non-planarity of graphs

acronyms.expansion: Complete bipartite graph with 3 vertices in each part - non-planar
Chi of G - Chromatic number of graph G
acronyms.usage: Represents minimum colours needed for proper colouring

visualTips.visualization: Imagine drawing on a balloon - if you can draw without pen lifting and lines crossing, it's planar
Think of a political map where countries share borders like puzzle pieces - only 4 crayons needed!
V - E + F = 2 is like: Vertices (corners) - Edges (lines) + Faces (spaces) = 2 for any connected planar shape
Turn each country into a dot; connect dots if countries touch - that's your dual graph

analogies.analogy: Like colouring states in USA - you never need more than 4 colours to ensure no two adjacent states share a colour
It's like the minimum number of friends you need to invite so no two know each other - graph's social network!
A subway map drawn on paper without tracks crossing - that's a planar graph!
Think of a connected path of same-coloured states in a map - you can swap colours along this chain
